original_text: Access to clean water becomes universal.
identified_property: quality and reliability of water access

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: Access to water meeting only minimal safety thresholds becomes universal, with frequently intermittent supply and inconsistent quality.

variations.label: low
variations.text: Access to basic safe water becomes universal, though supply reliability varies and quality occasionally falls below recommended standards.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: Access to clean water becomes universal, though delivery infrastructure is not fully reliable and service is occasionally disrupted.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: Access to clean water becomes universal.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: Access to rigorously purified, reliably delivered clean water becomes universal.

variations.label: high
variations.text: Access to high-purity, continuously monitored clean water with resilient delivery infrastructure becomes universal.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: Access to optimally purified, mineral-balanced water with guaranteed uninterrupted supply and real-time quality assurance becomes universal.

quality_checks.reasoning: All tiers describe universal water access. The property varied is exclusively the quality of the water (minimal safety → optimal purification and mineral balance) and the reliability of delivery (intermittent/inconsistent → guaranteed uninterrupted with real-time monitoring). Each tier adds a meaningful improvement in one or both dimensions without changing the universal scope.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because it combines the highest water purity (optimally purified, mineral-balanced), the strongest reliability guarantee (uninterrupted supply), and the most robust quality assurance (real-time monitoring), giving every person the best possible water access.
